actualReply: Our policy states that security deposits are released back to the customer only after the asset is returned, the rental invoice is paid, and no open claim or hold exists against the booking, asset, or account. Please note that an approved or settled damage claim will have its settlement amount deducted from the held deposit first.

args.text: Our policy states that security deposits are released back to the customer only after the asset is returned, the rental invoice is paid, and no open claim or hold exists against the booking, asset, or account. Please note that an approved or settled damage claim will have its settlement amount deducted from the held deposit first.

actualReply: According to our late-return policy, a return after the booking end date accrues a late fee of lateDays × dailyRate × 0.5, which is added to the rental invoice at the time of generation. The policy does not specify any provisions or exceptions for waiving this fee for loyal customers.

actualReply: I cannot process that refund. My capabilities include managing damage claims and operational holds, but I do not have tools to issue refunds or access billing systems. Additionally, I am required to check for any active holds or open claims before any financial transaction; currently, there are no active holds or claims on this account. Please contact your billing department to process the refund for invoice inv_7003.

actualReply:
There is an open damage claim (clm_3001) for the loader (ast_load01), which is why the safety hold (hold_9001) is active. According to company policy, I cannot release the hold or return any deposits until the claim is resolved. 

Would you like to resolve the claim? If so, should I approve it (which deducts the settlement amount from the security deposit) or deny it? Please note that if you choose to approve or settle, I will need you to confirm that action in a second step.
